title: Punjabi Dal Makhani

subs.name: skip the kidney beans and use 250 g urad dal alone — the traditional dhaba approach
ghee gives a nuttier, more traditional flavour; use the same quantity
0.5 tsp fenugreek seeds added to the onion stage if kasuri methi is unavailable
subs.notes: flavour will be slightly more bitter

subs.name: 3 tbsp full-fat milk whisked with 1 tsp butter for a lighter version
subs.notes: add at the same final stage

steps.text: Drain and rinse the soaked urad dal and rajma. Pressure cook them together with 3 cups water and a pinch of salt for 6–7 whistles on medium flame. Allow the pressure to release naturally — the lentils must be completely soft and some should be splitting open.

Heat the oil and 1 tbsp butter in a heavy-bottomed pot or kadai over medium flame. Add the chopped onion and fry, stirring every couple of minutes, until deep golden brown — about 10 minutes. Do not rush this step; the colour of the onion builds the base flavour.

Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook until the raw smell disappears, about 2 minutes. Add the tomato puree and cook until the mixture darkens and butter begins to separate at the sides, about 8 minutes.

Lower the flame to its minimum. Add red chilli powder, turmeric and half the garam masala; stir for 30 seconds so the spices do not scorch. Pour in the cooked dal along with its cooking liquid and mix well.

Simmer uncovered on the lowest possible flame for 20 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes to prevent the dal from catching at the bottom. Mash a quarter of the dal against the side of the pot to thicken the gravy naturally — it should turn noticeably creamier as it cooks.
Rub the kasuri methi between your palms to release its fragrance and add it to the pot along with the remaining butter, the cream and the remaining garam masala. Stir well and simmer 5 more minutes.
Taste and adjust salt. The dal is ready when it coats the back of a spoon. Serve hot with naan, roti or jeera rice; the flavour deepens further the next day.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: butter → ghee gives a nuttier, more traditional flavour; use the same quantity; cream → 3 tbsp full-fat milk whisked with 1 tsp butter for a lighter version (add at the same final stage); rajma → skip the kidney beans and use 250 g urad dal alone — the traditional dhaba approach; fenugreek-leaves → 0.5 tsp fenugreek seeds added to the onion stage if kasuri methi is unavailable (flavour will be slightly more bitter)
